Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

How did gurus upload their worship before we had mobile Internet?

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2009-06-29 · 1 answers
"When one has attained the topmost position of mahuc0u257 -bhu257 gavata, he is to be accepted as a guru and worshiped exactly like Hari, the Personality of Godhead. Only such a person is eligible to occupy the post of a guru." http://vedabase.net/cc/madhya/24/330/en

[img]http://lh4.ggpht.com/_yEpsW7PjOI8/ScNlNWd7XBI/AAAAAAAABNE/f7wTYCk_Bis/s800/image312.jpg[/img]
http://www.igor-film.org/ibda.html

I guess hes uploading the worship using file attachments, or is it instant messaging?
Note the pictures on the link above, hes on the cell as his feet are bathed with flowers, on the cell as his feet are bathed with yogurt (?), on the cell as his feet are bathed with honey, on the cell as his feet are bathed with milk, on the cell as his feet are bathed with water (?), on the cell as his feet are bathed with fruit juice (?)... I wonder how gurus did it before there was mobile internet.
user [313] · 2009-06-29
This is simply too much.
Anyway, Krsna knows. Everything is exactly as it has to be.
Lessons will be learnt, everybody gets what they need to move forward.
